title: React Dynamic Routing – Handling Variable URLs

meta.title: Mastering Dynamic Routing in React – URL Parameters & Query Strings (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to build flexible navigation with Dynamic Routing. Master useParams, useSearchParams, and pattern matching for profiles, products, and search results.

content.value: Dynamic routing allows you to define a single route path that can match multiple URLs based on a pattern. Instead of creating a separate route for every user or product, you use placeholders (params) to capture values from the URL and use them to fetch specific data.
To define a dynamic segment, you prefix the path part with a colon `:`. React Router will then capture whatever value is in that position and make it available via the `useParams` hook.

Query strings are the key-value pairs at the end of a URL (e.g., `/search?query=react&sort=asc`). Unlike path params, these are optional and handled via the `useSearchParams` hook, which works similarly to `useState`.

value: **Filtering**: Using query strings for sort order, price ranges, and search filters.

rows: Required for the route to match

faq.question: What happens if a user visits /product/ without an ID?
faq.answer: If the route is defined as /product/:id, it will NOT match. You would need to define a separate route for /product or use optional parameters if supported by your router version.

faq.question: Can I have multiple dynamic segments?
faq.answer: Yes. You can define routes like /authors/:authorId/posts/:postId and destructure both from useParams.
